title: Lachlan Fitzgibbon
wikitext:
{{Short description|Australian rugby league footballer (born 1994)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2026}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Lachlan Fitzgibbon |fullname = |image = Lachlan Fitzgibbon.jpg |imagesize = 290 |caption = Fitzgibbon in 2016 |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=yes|1994|01|05}} |birth_place = [[North Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia |height = {{convert|192|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|107|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} |position = {{rlp|SR}} |club1 = [[Newcastle Knights]] |year1start = 2015 |year1end = 23 |appearances1 = 119 |tries1 = 32 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 128 |club2 = [[Warrington Wolves]] |year2start = 2024 |year2end = 25 |appearances2 = 41 |tries2 = 2 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 8 |source = {{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/lachlan-fitzgibbon/summary.html|title=Lachlan Fitzgibbon - Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=|access-date=2016-03-12}} |updated = 14 March 2025 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Joel Fitzgibbon]] (uncle)
[[Eric Fitzgibbon]] (grandfather) }} }} '''Lachlan Fitzgibbon''' (born 5 January 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[South Newcastle Lions]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]. He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Fitzgibbon was born in [[North Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia and moved to [[Newcastle, New South Wales|Newcastle]] at a young age. He played his junior rugby league for the [[South Newcastle Lions]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]. He was then signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Fitzgibbon is the son of [[nib Health Funds|nib]] CEO Mark Fitzgibbon{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3254559/fitzgibbon-sees-light-at-end-of-tunnel/|title=Fitzgibbon sees light at end of tunnel|first=JOSH|last=LEESON|date=3 August 2015|website=Newcastle Herald|access-date=11 June 2019}} and nephew of [[Australian Labor Party]] MP [[Joel Fitzgibbon]]. Lachlan's grandfather is [[Eric Fitzgibbon]] who was an MP for the electorate of Hunter. ==Playing career== [[File:ESAN MARSTERS.jpg|right|thumb|Fitzgibbon attempting to tackle [[Esan Marsters]] of the [[Wests Tigers]] in 2017]] ===Early career=== In 2013 and 2014, Fitzgibbon played for the Newcastle Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/f/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304035513/http://nycdatabase.org/players/f/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=4 March 2016|title=F|work=NYC DATABASE|date=27 December 2012|access-date=2015-08-02}} ===2015=== In 2015, Fitzgibbon moved on to the Knights' [[New South Wales Cup]] team.{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/04/07/vb_nsw_cup_teams_rd_.html|title=VB NSW CUP TEAMS RD 6|publisher=NSWRL|date=7 April 2015|access-date=2015-08-02}} In round 21 of the [[2015 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Knights against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/08/02/late_mail_dragons_v_.html|title=LATE MAIL: Dragons v Knights – Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|date=2 August 2015|access-date=2015-08-02}} This would be Fitzgibbon's only appearance for the first grade team in the [[2015 NRL season]] as the club finished last on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/09/05/panthers-hand-knights-the-wooden-spoon/|title=Panthers hand Knights the wooden spoon|website=NRL|date=5 September 2015 }} In August, Fitzgibbon re-signed with the Newcastle club on a two-year contract.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/hot-property-moses-mbye-to-command-750000-per-season-nrl-market-watch/story-fn2mcuj6-1227479093112?from=public_rss|title=Hot Property Moses Mbye to Command 750,000 per Season|publisher=Foxpsorts.com.au|access-date=2015-10-24}}{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.nbnnews.com.au/2015/08/11/coaching-interviews-begin/|title=Coaching Interviews Begin|publisher=NBN News|date=|access-date=2015-08-11}} On 27 September, he played in the Knights' [[2015 New South Wales Cup#Chart|2015 New South Wales Cup Grand Final]] win over the [[Wyong Roos]] at [[Parramatta Stadium]].{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/09/22/vb_nsw_cup_grand_fin.html|title=VB NSW Cup Grand Final Team List|publisher=NSWRL|date=22 September 2015|access-date=2015-10-15}}{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/newcastle-knights-extend-clint-newtons-career-with-nsw-cup-win-over-wyong-roos-20150927-gjw2gs.html|title=Newcastle Knights extend Clint Newton's career with NSW Cup win over Wyong Roos|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=27 September 2015|access-date=2015-10-15}} ===2016=== Fitzgibbon made five appearances for Newcastle in the [[2016 NRL season]] as the club finished last for a second consecutive year. He scored his first try in the top grade against [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] in round 13 of the competition.{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/sione-matautia-says-the-time-has-come-for-the-knights-young-guns-to-deliver/news-story/7b921b828e8dfc97500dde3e9d08bb8b/|title=Sione Mata'utia says the time has come for the Knights young guns to deliver|website=Daily Telegraph}}{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}} ===2017=== Fitzgibbon made his first NRL appearance of the [[2017 NRL season]] in round 10, going on to play in 14 matches and scoring 8 tries as the club finished last for a third straight season.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMjE4NTQtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=6 September 2017}} In September, he re-signed with Newcastle on a two-year contract until the end of 2019.{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/lachlan-fitzgibbon-re-signs-knights-29125/|title=Lachlan Fitzgibbon re-signs with Knights - Zero Tackle|date=6 September 2017|publisher=|access-date=6 September 2017}}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/12/12/knights-2017-by-the-numbers/|title=Knights: 2017 by the numbers|website=NRL|date=12 December 2017 }} ===2018=== Fitzgibbon made 21 appearances and scored nine tries for Newcastle in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/05/newcastle-knights-2018-season-review/|title=Newcastle Knights 2018 season review|website=NRL|date=22 October 2018 }} ===2019=== Fitzgibbon played 21 games and scored five tries for Newcastle in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished a disappointing 11th on the table. At the start of the year, many tipped Newcastle to reach the finals after the club recruited heavily in the off-season.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/newcastle-knights-2019-preview-draw-squad-changes-news-every-player-odds-ticketing/c5drq2vctw2p1bq1oj9rmspwc/|title=Newcastle Knights: 2019 preview, draw, squad changes, news, every player, odds and ticketing|website=Sporting News|date=20 February 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://nothingbutleague.com/2019/10/03/2019-season-review-newcastle-knights/|title=2019 SEASON REVIEW NEWCASTLE KNIGHT|website=Nothing But League|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=3 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200203133211/https://nothingbutleague.com/2019/10/03/2019-season-review-newcastle-knights/|url-status=dead}} ===2020=== He made 17 appearances for Newcastle in the [[2020 NRL season]] including the club's first finals game since 2013 which was an elimination finals loss against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/11/29/knights-2020-season-by-the-numbers/|title=2020 season by the numbers|website=www.nrl.com|date=28 November 2020 }} ===2021=== Fitzgibbon only 10 games for Newcastle in the [[2021 NRL season]] and did not play in the club's elimination finals loss against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-12/nrl-parramatta-eels-defeat-newcastle-knights/100436668/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat Newcastle Knights 28-20 in NRL elimination final|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 September 2021}} ===2022=== Fitzgibbon was limited to only nine appearances for Newcastle in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-newcastle-knights-season-review/zuanch2lkdlbojvv3rtaafcg/|title=NRL 2022: Newcastle Knights season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=6 September 2022 }} ===2023=== Fitzgibbon made his 100th NRL appearance in March 2023 scoring a try for Newcastle in their 14–12 win over the [[Wests Tigers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/03/12/depleted-knights-hold-on-for-inspired-win-over-the-tigers/ |title=Depleted Knights hold on for inspired win over the Tigers |publisher=Newcastle Knights |date=12 March 2023 |access-date=12 December 2023}} In July, Fitzgibbon signed a three-year contract with [[Super League]] side [[Warrington Wolves]], starting in 2024.{{Cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23645820.lachlan-fitzgibbon-rules-early-warrington-wolves-move/|title=What Lachlan Fitzgibbon has said about bringing his Wire move forward|date=10 July 2023|website=Warrington Guardian}} ===2024=== On 8 June, Fitzgibbon played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=8 June 2024 }} Fitzgibbon played 14 matches for Warrington in the 2024 Super League season as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=17 October 2024 }} ===2025=== On 7 June 2025, Fitzgibbon played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} Fitzgibbon played 24 games for Warrington in the 2025 Super League season as they missed the playoffs finishing 8th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} === 2026 === Fitzgibbon following his release from Warrington, signed a deal with the South Newcastle Lions for the 2026 season.{{Cite web |last=Issa |first=Isaac |date=2026-01-20 |title=Former NRL forward joins new club |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/former-nrl-forward-joins-new-club-230060/ |access-date=2026-01-20 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}} ==
